close_reason: OS keychain APIs handle name length limits; pre-validation would duplicate platform-specific rules
description:
Windows Credential Manager limits TargetName to 32767 wide chars and credential blob to ~512 bytes; macOS Keychain Services has its own attribute-length limits. keyring 3 maps these to Error::TooLong(name, len). secretx-desktop currently does not pre-validate at from_uri(); the caller learns at first get/put/JSON parse-time that their service or account name was too long.

Impact: confusing failure mode for users — they construct a backend successfully, then it fails later with a backend-specific error.

Severity: low. Pre-validating cross-platform limits accurately is itself a maintenance burden (the limits differ between macOS/Windows/SecretService).

Recommend: do NOT add proactive length checks. Document in module docs that platform limits apply and TooLong surfaces as Backend. This is a 'document, don't validate' finding.
title: Service/account length limits not validated at construction
